After the meeting, he submitted three of his RNC resolutions to be incorporated into the 2024 RNC National Platform. I know we often feel unheard here in deep blue Oregon. Solomon is making sure the voice of Oregon's grassroots helps shape the direction of the RNC. The process took two years to achieve. President Trump has already talked about these policy positions during his media interviews and at his rallies. The party also hopes our congressional and senatorial nominees run on these issues. These new planks will be based on the new resolution, and also these two: ~ Among other meetings, I attended the RNC Western Region breakfast meeting. An election was held for the RNC Contest Committee. This committee adjudicates challenges to delegations at the 2024 National Convention, so the members have to be known and respected for their honesty and integrity. Hawaii’s NCW Laura Nakanelua was nominated last-minute from the floor and won. Oregon’s current NCW Tracy Honl had been vying for the position and was soundly defeated. Laura will do a fabulous job! It looked to me as if most of the regions leaned toward solid, grassroots-supporting members in their elections. NCM/NCW RACES In Oregon, the next election for National Committeeman and National Committeewoman happens March 16. More on that next time! There will be several big turnovers in the membership of the 168 in the next few months. My observation is that some of the members are there for the photo ops, food, and flowers ... while Oregon and America deserve better. Over & Out, Kerry | | | ~ | | The Baker County Republican Women's Annual Banquet is April 20th. Funds raised go to launch our future Patriot Kids Camp, teaching real American history, not a woke narrative! Learn more and ~ | | | | | | Do you want Oregon to be America's next constitutional carry state? Have you signed the IP-21 petition yet? If not, please download, sign, and send in the single-signer sheet. CCFO also has autographed copies of my friend Kari's Lake's book** for sale if you'd like to help the cause. | | | | ~~ "KerryMcQuisten.com" You received this email because you subscribed to our list. If a friend or family member subscribed for you and you don't wish to receive these newsletters, you can: ~~ at any time. name: Tom Fitton
subject: This is What We’re Up Against!
body:
The Left is now pulling out all the stops across the country to overturn election safeguards, and make it easier for non-citizens and other ineligible people to vote.

| | |
---
|
---
|
---
| Dear Fellow American,

**The Left is now pulling out all the stops across the country to overturn election safeguards, and make it easier for non-citizens and other ineligible people to vote.**
_And I have a critical question for you_ :
**_Do you believe that Judicial Watch 's full range of election integrity work...lawsuits, research and investigations... _should be our top priority for 2024_?_**
I need to know your opinion!
If you think our election integrity work _should_ be our top priority, [I urge you to let me know and make a contribution today](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39) to help us take on all the election challenges confronting us!
| [Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39)
---

Since 2012, we've fought successfully to protect honest election safeguards...like going to bat for states defending their voter ID laws; and forcing states to make reasonable efforts to keep their voter rolls accurate...work the Justice Department should actually be doing..._but_ _isn 't_.

Our lawsuits and settlements have resulted in the removal of more than **4 MILLION** deceased, non-citizen, moved or otherwise ineligible voters from voter lists in more than a dozen red states _and_ blue states across the country.

That's not all...

* We've provided expert testimony on a number of occasions before **Congress** in opposition to leftist power grabs (led by Biden administration officials and a network of Soros groups) to "federalize" our elections and put the corrupt Justice Department in control of administering elections.

* We successfully sued **Maryland** and overturned the Democratic legislature's hyper-partisan, election-rigging "gerrymander" scheme.

* We're in **Illinois** fighting in federal court to block the state's efforts to open the floodgates to voter fraud by allowing mail-in ballots to arrive and be counted up to 14 days after Election Day!

* And we recently filed, with the Allied Educational Foundation, a "friend of the court" brief in the **U.S.** **Supreme Court** in support of former President Trump's challenge to the Colorado Supreme Court's unprecedented decision to remove him from the 2024 presidential primary ballot.

We find that the American legal system, though slow at times, works best in helping protect fair and honest elections from the Left's attempts to corrupt them.

[That's why I want to know](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39) if you believe that our full range of election integrity work...lawsuits, research and investigations... should be our _top_ _priority_ for 2024.

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39)
---

The question is particularly timely today because we are reviewing more data to determine if additional "clean voter registration rolls" lawsuits are needed to protect safeguards against votes being cast by non-citizens and other ineligible individuals.

Personally, I believe that Judicial Watch should continue to take up every challenge related to keeping our elections honest...because if we don't, who will?

[**What do you think?**](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39)

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39)
---

**I want to know that you and other members share our commitment to the absolute necessity of our election integrity work and are willing to support our work with[a special tax-deductible contribution today](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39), because we need to be sure we have the resources to add more investigations and lawsuits with jeopardizing any current litigation.**

Thanks to your unfailing commitment, your Judicial Watch is the largest and most effective government watchdog group in the nation. We're always fighting...and often winning...

We work hard every day. We dig in for the long haul. And we often win key battles in the election integrity war, and also come away with key information about what our government is up to!

Thank you for helping us perform this essential work.

Sincerely,

Thomas Fitton
President

[Contribute](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=66954&L=30946&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvZG9uYXRlL3RoYW5rLXlvdS0yMTYvP3V0bV9jb250ZW50PUNNamN3T0RrNU5UUSUzRA==&hash=28479272f3d75e2e0dc970ca9541d23cd492cd848ed6f14cb6d0e89c2ff9bc39)
---

---
|

Washington, DC 20024

clean_body: | = Peter – = One week ago, we CRUSHED our January goal. But, in the days since, my team told me donations have ticked down a bit. I was really surprised to hear that – the enthusiasm I’m seeing on the campaign trail is amazing. Folks here in Tennessee are FIRED UP to defeat Marsha Blackburn – and voters are clearly sick and tired of right-wing politicians playing games with their democracy. So I can’t believe our fundraising isn’t keeping pace. Look, Marsha Blackburn has a massive campaign war chest she spent years building. I’m going to need the support of thousands of grassroots donors if I’m going to have the resources I need to compete. That’s why I’m reaching out to you right now. = | = Thank you, Gloria Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address and sometimes takes up to a week to sync in our systems. If you have donated — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign. | Gloria Johnson is running for the U.S. Senate because Tennesseans deserve leaders who care. For too long, elected officials like Marsha Blackburn have done absolutely nothing to make Tennesseans’ lives better. The only interests they’ve cared about are special interests. The only people they’ve championed are their donors. Gloria will advocate for real solutions to protect our communities from gun violence, make health care more affordable and accessible for all of us, secure access to the ballot box, and defend reproductive rights. | = = Follow along: | | ---|---|--- Paid for by Gloria for Tennessee Contributions or gifts to Gloria for Tennessee are not tax deductible. Send donations via check to: Gloria for Tennessee 73 White Bridge Rd #103-353 Nashville, TN 37205

As of this week, EMILYs List has _**four**_ new House endorsements on our roster. Our new endorsements are joining 31 Democratic pro-choice women running to flip the House this November and protect reproductive freedom nationwide.
* As a fifth generation Northern Californian,**Jessica Morse (CA-03)** has deep roots in the 3rd District. She's dedicated her career to protecting our national security while working at the State Department and Defense Department, and we know she has what it takes to flip this seat in November.

* Palo Alto Councilmember **Julie Lythcott-Haims (CA-16)** is running to fill an open seat. Julie is a parent, educator, lawyer, former Stanford dean, and an author. If elected, she would be the first openly LGBTQ+ Black woman elected to Congress.

* **Luz Maria Rivas (CA-29)** is an engineer, educator, and public servant from the San Fernando Valley running to fill an open seat. She's served in the California State Legislature since 2018, where her priority has been to uplift and empower underrepresented communities.

* State senator and practicing OB-GYN **Kelly Morrison (MN-03)** is running for an open seat. She's focused on expanding the progress that Minnesota has made on abortion rights. As a physician, she knows how important it is that medical decisions are made between patients and their doctors — not the government.

clean_body: We're raising $75,000 to help Democratic pro-choice women FLIP the House! | = Peter, Today we're launching a 48-hour Flash Fund to help EMILYs List women FLIP the House, including four just-announced new endorsements! We'll tell you more about these incredible women in a moment.= | = As of this week, EMILYs List has four new House endorsements on our roster. Our new endorsements are joining 31 Democratic pro-choice women running to flip the House this November and protect reproductive freedom nationwide. As a fifth generation Northern Californian,Jessica Morse (CA-03) has deep roots in the 3rd District. She's dedicated her career to protecting our national security while working at the State Department and Defense Department, and we know she has what it takes to flip this seat in November. Palo Alto Councilmember Julie Lythcott-Haims (CA-16) is running to fill an open seat. Julie is a parent, educator, lawyer, former Stanford dean, and an author. If elected, she would be the first openly LGBTQ+ Black woman elected to Congress. Luz Maria Rivas (CA-29) is an engineer, educator, and public servant from the San Fernando Valley running to fill an open seat. She's served in the California State Legislature since 2018, where her priority has been to uplift and empower underrepresented communities. State senator and practicing OB-GYN Kelly Morrison (MN-03) is running for an open seat. She's focused on expanding the progress that Minnesota has made on abortion rights. As a physician, she knows how important it is that medical decisions are made between patients and their doctors — not the government. It's absolutely critical that we flip the House this cycle in order to protect and restore abortion rights. We're proud to endorse these women because we know they're up to the challenge. Will you donate before midnight tomorrow to join the fight and help these 35 EMILYs List candidates flip the House this November? If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue, your donation will go through immediately: = | = ---|--- = | = = | = Thank you, the EMILYs List Campaigns team EMILYs List is a community of over five million members that helps elect Democratic pro-choice women to office. = = Paid for by EMILYs List and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: Powered By People
subject: Can you help us get back on the road?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE
body:
[1]Powered By People

We wanted to make sure you saw Beto’s email about the organizing drives
that we held across the state last week. They were so successful at
recruiting Volunteer Deputy Registrars (VDRs) that we’re already raising
funds to do another round of events soon!

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240207.hvlL0z&refcode=ema20240207-239079&t=1001&akid=239079%2E444211%2E5PLfzl ]Can you chip in whatever you can today to help us book event space and
put gas in Beto’s truck?

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240207.hvlL0z&refcode=ema20240207-239079&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=239079%2E444211%2E5PLfzl ]DONATE $3

Your support will ensure we can recruit the volunteer army we need to
register as many voters as possible in the months ahead.

— Powered by People

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Email from Beto from February 5, 2024 is forwarded below:

We’re pushing hard to bring new voters onto the rolls so that we can win
critical elections in Texas. And while we’ve been successful in recruiting
volunteers to do this work through online events, nothing beats being on
the ground.

So last week, I left El Paso to lead organizing drives across the state.
We started off in Dallas and Fort Worth, then continued on to Austin, San
Antonio, and Houston. And I’ve got to tell you — the response truly
exceeded my expectations.

I was shocked not only by how big volunteer turnout was in each city, but
by how many people signed up for the toughest work of becoming a Volunteer
Deputy Registrar (VDR) with our team.

VDRs are essentially “super volunteers” who are the heart of our program.
To become one, you have to take a training, pass an exam, and get
deputized by your county elections office. Once you’re deputized, you can
legally register Texas voters.

Since Texas Republicans have banned online, automatic, and same-day voter
registration, these volunteers are the heroes we need in this moment and
the key to ensuring that every eligible voter can participate in our
elections.

And they’re already getting after it, nine months out from the general
election:

In 2020, Powered by People registered more than 200,000 voters and helped
produce the highest turnout that Texas had seen in nearly three decades.
And we did it with less than a third of the VDRs who have signed up with
us so far in 2024 — so just imagine what we can accomplish this year if we
keep up the momentum.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240207.hvlL0z&refcode=ema20240207-239079&t=1021&akid=239079%2E444211%2E5PLfzl ]I hope you’ll support this work with a donation of $3
or whatever you can today. Every dollar will go toward recruiting more
volunteers, registering more voters, and increasing Democratic turnout in
critical elections across the state.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20240207.hvlL0z&refcode=ema20240207-239079&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1040&akid=239079%2E444211%2E5PLfzl ]DONATE $3

We’re off to a great start. So let’s keep it going, and push even harder
if we can. Winning Texas is a matter of time and effort. The more effort
we put in, the less time it will take to get there.

The people of this country are counting on us.

Beto

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1041&akid=239079%2E444211%2E5PLfzl

PAID FOR BY POWERED BY PEOPLE

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Our current health care system is incredibly complex, fragmented, and expensive
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
I support universal health care because nobody should be denied care they need or go bankrupt to pay for it. Too many Americans are one medical bill away from disaster.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78585112/453825375/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyMTg1MjM0OC1lOGM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjM4NDgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=IUYFG7adcyQ3wcLE1-7zfT7DpK9t3xPBhwxtApwlM9E=&emci=90ef0ddd-dfc5-ee11-b660-002248223848&emdi=21852348-e8c5-ee11-b660-002248223848&ceid=7731215)
---

| | Health care is a right, not a privilege. I support universal health care because nobody should be denied the care they need or go bankrupt to pay for it.**Too many Americans are one medical bill away from disaster.** Our current health care system is incredibly confusing, fragmented, and expensive. Americans could have so much more peace of mind if we had a simple, seamless system where, solely by virtue of living in America, you knew you would get the care you needed for life regardless of background or income. That's why last year, I joined several of my colleagues to reintroduce Medicare for All in the Senate to get the debate started once again. Here's why we need Medicare for All:
* **69% of adults in the U.S. support providing Medicare to every American, and studies estimate Medicare for All would save $650 billion and 68,000 lives per year.**
* While 44% of American adults struggle to pay for needed medical care, the country's seven major health insurance companies raked in over $69 billion in profits in 2022.
* As millions of American families face bankruptcy and financial ruin because of the outrageously high cost of health care, the CEOs of 300 major health care companies collectively cashed in on $4.5 billion in total compensation in 2021.
* While one out of four Americans cannot afford the life-saving medicine their doctors prescribe, in 2022 ten of the top pharmaceutical companies in the United States made over $112 billion in profits, and the top 50 executives in these companies made a combined $1.5 billion in total compensation.

It's time we put people before corporate profits and pass Medicare for All, once and for all! Onward! _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:

[SB 39](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkBbnzZIE-gzHEYrmqFU8oVMVJXYXkfKdzy822Z2_w9LDsMeE5XQ1kJ0sdmAbJJVa8m_uj5hI_4f_iWO-GrrlUqovlaXoiVCS1X7V1lu-nxAsPDLZqBOKLh6wsyluMMBIne0W8q0mXx6JBk2LvcwWZ68=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Favola Kinship as Foster Care Prevention Program; established.
[SB 118](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRczBdbjUNJlO_zMHf0xpmS00-OgCipg74C6or0rBZBopdT5rXum5uqc_H1LlAbYoSW2qpD61LgGDFlGI1zADrzM3kl7ASY0YY6VdQDAQHnITin--xR1uTCRBbx7RFE6-vu5u53UqGVTHdA=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Locke Health insurance; coverage for doula care services.[SB 186](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkND5x21qoB9OQxymVo1WUwI-lt4SqsjjOjFcWkrMyOj8B1v6SVqI5JRUYt8Na6Hbf4bVOm7k-XBeUwPBt0ZUeQQlbxGVEwLFGjNNp1EeiqOsn_zWbzqDC6HLjH1li2sANKfYekmpFpbViXEhTu-6Ioc=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==)Subramanyam Wholesale prescription drug importation program; Sec. of Health and Human Resources to establish.

---
| [S.B. 37](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkFCa1E5a_XeRgXpXD4XRW-pBN907QQaN39X279V3MLfFYhdxb3R7LPGLo5k-ESdNYUcNqgBP6zqYdhTpXF--a15GKL1a06NMKmvOmfIyWS4gIwuCoiHPUh5NUg7k0dmhpK40J50nkvfpB27giM4h_Lc=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: McGuire Sage's Law; minor students experiencing gender incongruence; parental notification of certain expressions and requests and parental permission for certain plans required; parental care. Requires each public elementary or secondary school principal or his designee to (i) as soon as practicable, inform at least one parent of a minor student enrolled in such school if such minor (a) expresses to any individual who is employed in such school that such minor is experiencing gender incongruence, as defined in the bill, or (b) requests that any such employee participate in social affirmation of such minor's gender incongruence or the transition of such minor to a sex or gender different from the minor's biological sex while at school and (ii) request and receive permission from at least one parent of a minor student enrolled at such school prior to the implementation at such school of any plan concerning any gender incongruence experienced by such minor, including any counseling of such minor at school. Any such plan shall include provision for parental participation to the extent requested by the parent. The bill also clarifies, in the definition of the term "abused or neglected child," that in no event shall referring to and raising the child in a manner consistent with the child's biological sex, including related mental health or medical decisions, be considered abuse or neglect.
[S.B. 68](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkFCa1E5a_XeR4XGhsBvFeg0a-i89mCB6DHwlRz29XBLlvoHkzohAMjTGkow-S3AspwfZ35aMtO5NAwFdOkYYGUl0urXmQjTX8qLD-_dIOrVjKfHy-3OGw-TqzLeg5rgcJMzxlj-cNPmRvuWerkc69xk=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Peake Elementary and secondary schools; athletics; participation in female sports; civil cause of action. Requires each public elementary or secondary school and each private elementary or secondary school that competes in sponsored athletic events against such public schools to designate all interscholastic athletic teams and intramural athletic teams sponsored by such school based on biological sex as follows: (i) for "males," "men," or "boys"; (ii) for "females," "women," or "girls"; or (iii) as "coed" or "mixed" if such team is open to participation by (a) "males," "men," or "boys" and (b) "females," "women," or "girls." The bill prohibits any student whose biological sex is male and who has not physically transitioned to female prior to puberty from participating on any school athletic team or squad designated for "females," "women," or "girls." Finally, the bill creates a civil cause of action for students and schools that suffer harm as a result of a violation of the provisions of the bill, provided that such action is initiated within two years of the harm occurring.
[S.B. 220](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRcz_81_T6oNwtTH945NdErddsOj6IHtTxAnZmqPwS-lOyuoZ8pDD1loYtQy80fM2FIxB7S7bS8JLcxoxvDRMdxn8PV_DFgDuMmSEcR0lWzFfZ0HJq9AkG2WYycXtDQ1rPRVQiOSF6hAuNc=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Favola
Special education and related services; definitions; records retention; duties of Department of Education; parent engagement specialists; professional development; education preparation program coursework. Makes several changes relating to special education and related services for children with disabilities in public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth, including (i) establishing a definition for "individualized education program" or "IEP"; (ii) requiring, after a child with a disability graduates from, ages out of, or otherwise leaves any public elementary or secondary school in the Commonwealth, such school to retain the special education records of such child for at least 75 years; (iii) requiring each local school board to (a) provide high-quality professional development in instructional practices to support specially designed instruction in inclusive settings, as that term is defined in the bill, for certain teachers and administrators employed in the local school division and (b) employ at least one full-time equivalent special education parent engagement specialist to serve as a resource to parents to understand and engage in the referral, evaluation, and eligibility process if they suspect that their child has a disability and to understand and engage in the IEP process; (iv) requiring the Department of Education, among other things, to (a) establish eight regional special education parent support centers that are each staffed by a regional special education parent ombudsman and coordinate with such division special education parent engagement specialists employed by each local school board; (b) elevate the position of State Parent Ombudsman for Special Education to perform certain functions, including systematically tracking and reporting questions and concerns raised by parents to the Superintendent of Public Instruction and coordinating the activities of such regional special education parent support centers; and (c) develop, establish, review and update as necessary at least once every five years, make available to each local school board an IEP writing, facilitation, tracking, and transfer system to be referred to as the Virginia IEP; and (v) requiring each education preparation program offered by a public institution of higher education or private institution of higher education or alternative certification program that provides training for any student seeking initial licensure by the Board of Education with certain endorsements to include a program of coursework and require all such students to demonstrate mastery in instructional practices to support specially designed instruction in inclusive settings.
[S.B. 237](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkCNA9fb5jn0wHdaw2SS2nX6oyiP1ejLTCDGR2G6NSICS3XNJ_6Xi7J2_LGCzmu87e0a5WHXlkZQjwRLKFFWLHx_vFhtGvr1sLngoPBYFhPxEi-qupHBB0YuZlT7_0fUc8VA8aPHOcefvpYTOhFPd7l8=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Hashmi Contraception; right to contraception; applicability; enforcement. Establishes a right to obtain contraceptives and engage in contraception, as defined in the bill. The bill creates a cause of action that may be instituted against anyone who infringes on such right.
[S.B. 395](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkLl8qNK1XjWj7tvty_EmugCmVEatUq_jphHlOSjNELeCVH6-O8WTRCu8aG8fWGGiMCe9MUXRapp5fL37iXbD1XFrdTw0rrYMvwWzsv-HUlwYyf7GuYD2Jhy5jMnAvnU9pz0sB3E5AxS_DROWk_eY_-o=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Pekarsky Public elementary and secondary schools; student athletes; pre-participation mental health assessment required. Provides that no public elementary or secondary school student is permitted to be a participant on or try out for any school athletic team or squad with a predetermined roster, regular practices, and scheduled competitions with other elementary or secondary schools unless such student has submitted to the school principal a signed report from a licensed physician, licensed advanced practice registered nurse, or licensed physician assistant acting under the supervision of a licensed physician attesting that such student has, within the preceding 365 days, received both a physical examination and a mental health assessment. Current law only requires that the signed report attest that any such student has received a physical examination within the preceding 12 months. The bill prohibits any public elementary or secondary school from becoming a member of any organization or entity that regulates or governs interscholastic programs that does not deem eligible for participation any student who has satisfied the requirements for eligibility in accordance with the provisions of the bill. The provisions of the bill other than the requirement for the Board of Education to convene a work group have a delayed effective date of July 1, 2025.
[S.B. 440](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRcz2DGwytKRyK0AX6MZEIZ0rXzZyjEVoZJOp7Sj5Jo9-12x6nemFqyyUONdaZLAkDVpqG_qxlWWvt7Wll8YaPrG2Q_jBsTKaqOnonTNo3aScAtsJfrkXU7RmZjagF5w1niWhinYzI7ynbA=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Durant Employment of school protection officers in public schools. Permits any local law-enforcement agency to employ in any public elementary or secondary school in the local school division, pursuant to an agreement with the local school board, a school protection officer, defined in the bill as a retired law-enforcement officer hired by the local law-enforcement agency on a part-time basis to provide limited law-enforcement and security services to public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth. The bill requires each such school board and local law-enforcement agency to enter into a memorandum of understanding that sets forth the powers and duties of school protection officers. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish compulsory training standards for school protection officers and requires the collection of certain data relating to the activities of such officers.
[S.B. 499](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkP1w6jCe4M-LN0C11DNn725eTTuLUxkX_EVFFfdOO_lfwBBnjzHEcWH_w5LX7afx-FIHMVdoWWUrHhkQBrkOqEthwZzWH0lm1LSIisqz1wJFkteQlzYyDaxPt3JEtadNIEIf2UTruY--pUvoWtEaAro=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Carroll Foy Donor human milk banks, health insurance; coverage for donor human milk. Prohibits any person from establishing or operating a donor human milk bank without first obtaining a license from the Department of Health. The bill directs the Department to establish criteria for the licensure of donor human milk banks and to promulgate regulations by July 1, 2025, that prohibit activities of donor human milk banks that do not meet the licensing standards and requirements The bill requires health insurers, corporations providing health care coverage subscription contracts, and health maintenance organizations to provide coverage for expenses incurred in the provision of pasteurized donor human milk. The bill specifies that the requirement applies if the covered person is an infant younger than the age of six months and a licensed medical practitioner has issued an order for such infant who satisfies certain criteria enumerated in the bill. The bill applies to policies, contracts, and plans delivered, issued for delivery, or renewed on or after January 1, 2025. The bill also requires the state plan for medical assistance services to include a provision for payment of medical assistance services incurred in the provision of pasteurized donor human milk.
[S.B. 586](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRcz_fOJ1Afv87PVGOUSGHy1Hv1zFq-JQzI3QzyvM02pPKxpnKO8lTdrv_fUyCZyYDow8zLXqeCk2QpdBUZBo9X0aFrIxGTb2t1nhJG8AI7jxwIZoWxTPvzReBx8TObDG2ICTfmLNmvjPvY=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Pekarsky Public elementary and secondary schools; student discipline; evidence-based restorative disciplinary practices. Prohibits, except in certain cases involving firearms, drugs, adjudications of delinquency, or convictions of certain crimes, any public elementary or secondary school student from being suspended, expelled, or excluded from attendance at school without consideration of first implementing at least one evidence-based restorative disciplinary practice such as community conferencing, community service, mentoring, a peer jury, peer mediation, positive behavioral interventions and supports, a restorative circle, or the Virginia Tiered Systems of Supports.
[S.B. 723](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkFCa1E5a_XeR_6CMW5hftafr6-5cq1tolYDewTxzGcAp1qCn9FAo7o1MxJWXz38-HBLXWVzh7jLRyzvczfeLg2cWTiX1CTRJ66S3EfadRnl7UOI66hjJhKoOcSookSPDGqNwfG_TTfGAaP6GKFPlU9M=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Mulchi
K-12 schools and institutions of higher education; student participation in women's sports; civil cause of action. Requires each interscholastic, intercollegiate, intramural, or club athletic team or sport sponsored by a public school, or any other school that is a member of the Virginia High School League, or by a public institution of higher education to be expressly designated as one of the following based on the biological sex of the students who participate on the team or in the sport: (i) males, men, or boys; (ii) females, women, or girls; or (iii) coed or mixed if participation on such team or sport is open to both males and females. The bill prohibits any such team or sport that is expressly designated for females from being open to students whose biological sex is male. The bill also provides that in the event of a dispute as to the biological sex of any student seeking to participate on any interscholastic, intercollegiate, intramural, or club athletic team or sport that is expressly designated for males or females, such student may establish biological sex by presenting to the school or institution a signed physician's statement that attests to such student's biological sex based solely on (a) the student's internal and external reproductive anatomy; (b) the student's normal endogenously produced levels of testosterone; and (c) an analysis of the student's genetic makeup. The bill prohibits any government entity, licensing or accrediting organization, or athletic association or organization from entertaining a complaint, opening an investigation, or taking any other adverse action against any such school or institution of higher education based on a violation of the provisions of the bill and creates a cause of action for any school or institution of higher education that suffers harm as a result of a violation of the bill. Finally, the bill creates a civil cause of action for any student who suffers harm as a result of a knowing violation of a provision of the bill by a school or institution or as a result of the student's reporting a violation of a provision of the bill by a school, institution, athletic association, or organization.

| [S.B. 94](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRczJVg3fBKQEqVYu76ggPT8wLQ9abC0sZfXViMz5hD4UIr3FPA9xFSK1L1zONcu3fKicmbuA1lQXFNG9a3f579mUSWQFDg3JFXmYJnSqn4e094-1HQONHbUd3NI55tGqqXA1aHm5A6UK2M=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Stanley Tow truck drivers and towing and recovery operators; prohibited acts; certain solicitation and offering of services; penalty. Prohibits tow truck drivers and towing and recovery operators from soliciting or offering towing services or causing any other person to solicit or offer towing services in any manner, directly or indirectly, at the scene of any wrecked or disabled motor vehicle upon a highway when such wrecked or disabled motor vehicle reasonably necessitates removal by a tow truck. The bill provides that a violation of such prohibition constitutes a Class 1 misdemeanor.
[S.B. 539](https://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001-N2MCYswUKIhjZfImXElY6Z2FmfHcsQ_ydhvK3cPde2uxxro8PWOkJGyIN1gKRczR0e12bK82kYa2m6w59nBoL773rXQXv_b-5J7QwYlNlzcl6T6Bhvplq4YOvZbMVv_FL7VMsZjUZHJlKjO7C5M4ctefAoSPpAAF8FmqMMyLkGQzGRpGsUC0WBKa1xLSme4DnGNAf4wjsQ=&c=NorAX-a0BDcxEyMsEYa_vEq8HqxaQ71PTTHkRizb0RgGaM5eEBLXiA==&ch=nu4PfYxVl1BReqChbPwsrNIq3DAeXmQcFXYW_jkNJHni4ZnC-CTjzg==) Patron: Bagby License plate readers; penalties. Provides requirements for the use of license plate readers, defined in the bill, by law-enforcement agencies. The bill requires such agencies to enter into an agreement with the license plate reader owners or other responsible non-law-enforcement entity to operate a data trust, defined in the bill, to store the data collected by a license plate reader and requires any such law-enforcement agency to apply to the data trust for access to such data. The bill limits the use of license plate readers to scanning, detecting, and identifying license plate numbers for the purpose of identifying vehicles involved in certain crimes.

Friends, Each house has until next Tuesday, February 13 at midnight before crossover, when all bills must be voted on by each respective house.
Subcommittees are currently winding down or not meeting tomorrow as bills move from committee to the Senate floor for a final vote.
YOU can participate remotely with your legislators from the comfort of your home.
YOU can watch Senate Committees in action and at the right time, comment to the committee in real time.
This is YOUR opportunity to DO SOMETHING.
Above I have listed the dockets for THIS THURSDAY'S Senate Committee hearings. By clicking each link you can quickly look over which bills are coming up this Thursday that are of interest to you.

| Thank you to those who have pitched in to help cover my costs to communicate with you each week while the General Assembly is in session. Thank you.
Many of you have advised me that you appreciate my instructive and informative emails, regarding important bills coming before the Virginia General Assembly.
I'm delighted to continue to send you these emails and ask for your continued support so that I may continue to communicate with you.
If you haven't done so already, will you please help me offset the cost by pitching in a few dollars?
It costs about $400 per month to send out emails to keep you informed about bills coming before the General Assembly.
Would you please make a donation today to help offset the cost, so that I can continue to bring you the latest on what's going on in the General Assembly? After serving you for 8 years in the Virginia Senate, I know how things really work in Richmond and want to keep you informed.
Please consider donating $25, $50, $100 so that I can continue to communicate with you and help you to engage and interact with your representatives to ensure accountability and transparency.

clean_body: Participate online or in person here | | | | Senate Bills Up THURSDAY CALL IN REMOTELY OR SHOW UP | | Senate Committee Public Hearings for THURSDAY Thursday's Dockets BILLS TO WATCH | BILLS TO WATCH THURSDAY | | == | == Deeds Prescription Drug Affordability Board; established, drug cost affordability review, report. == Favola Kinship as Foster Care Prevention Program; established. == Locke Health insurance; coverage for doula care services. Wholesale prescription drug importation program; Sec. of Health and Human Resources to establish. | | == | == Patron: McGuire Sage's Law; minor students experiencing gender incongruence; parental notification of certain expressions and requests and parental permission for certain plans required; parental care. Requires each public elementary or secondary school principal or his designee to (i) as soon as practicable, inform at least one parent of a minor student enrolled in such school if such minor (a) expresses to any individual who is employed in such school that such minor is experiencing gender incongruence, as defined in the bill, or (b) requests that any such employee participate in social affirmation of such minor's gender incongruence or the transition of such minor to a sex or gender different from the minor's biological sex while at school and (ii) request and receive permission from at least one parent of a minor student enrolled at such school prior to the implementation at such school of any plan concerning any gender incongruence experienced by such minor, including any counseling of such minor at school. Any such plan shall include provision for parental participation to the extent requested by the parent. The bill also clarifies, in the definition of the term "abused or neglected child," that in no event shall referring to and raising the child in a manner consistent with the child's biological sex, including related mental health or medical decisions, be considered abuse or neglect. == Patron: Peake Elementary and secondary schools; athletics; participation in female sports; civil cause of action. Requires each public elementary or secondary school and each private elementary or secondary school that competes in sponsored athletic events against such public schools to designate all interscholastic athletic teams and intramural athletic teams sponsored by such school based on biological sex as follows: (i) for "males," "men," or "boys"; (ii) for "females," "women," or "girls"; or (iii) as "coed" or "mixed" if such team is open to participation by (a) "males," "men," or "boys" and (b) "females," "women," or "girls." The bill prohibits any student whose biological sex is male and who has not physically transitioned to female prior to puberty from participating on any school athletic team or squad designated for "females," "women," or "girls." Finally, the bill creates a civil cause of action for students and schools that suffer harm as a result of a violation of the provisions of the bill, provided that such action is initiated within two years of the harm occurring. == Patron: Favola Special education and related services; definitions; records retention; duties of Department of Education; parent engagement specialists; professional development; education preparation program coursework. Makes several changes relating to special education and related services for children with disabilities in public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth, including (i) establishing a definition for "individualized education program" or "IEP"; (ii) requiring, after a child with a disability graduates from, ages out of, or otherwise leaves any public elementary or secondary school in the Commonwealth, such school to retain the special education records of such child for at least 75 years; (iii) requiring each local school board to (a) provide high-quality professional development in instructional practices to support specially designed instruction in inclusive settings, as that term is defined in the bill, for certain teachers and administrators employed in the local school division and (b) employ at least one full-time equivalent special education parent engagement specialist to serve as a resource to parents to understand and engage in the referral, evaluation, and eligibility process if they suspect that their child has a disability and to understand and engage in the IEP process; (iv) requiring the Department of Education, among other things, to (a) establish eight regional special education parent support centers that are each staffed by a regional special education parent ombudsman and coordinate with such division special education parent engagement specialists employed by each local school board; (b) elevate the position of State Parent Ombudsman for Special Education to perform certain functions, including systematically tracking and reporting questions and concerns raised by parents to the Superintendent of Public Instruction and coordinating the activities of such regional special education parent support centers; and (c) develop, establish, review and update as necessary at least once every five years, make available to each local school board an IEP writing, facilitation, tracking, and transfer system to be referred to as the Virginia IEP; and (v) requiring each education preparation program offered by a public institution of higher education or private institution of higher education or alternative certification program that provides training for any student seeking initial licensure by the Board of Education with certain endorsements to include a program of coursework and require all such students to demonstrate mastery in instructional practices to support specially designed instruction in inclusive settings. == Patron: Hashmi Contraception; right to contraception; applicability; enforcement. Establishes a right to obtain contraceptives and engage in contraception, as defined in the bill. The bill creates a cause of action that may be instituted against anyone who infringes on such right. == Patron: Pekarsky Public elementary and secondary schools; student athletes; pre-participation mental health assessment required. Provides that no public elementary or secondary school student is permitted to be a participant on or try out for any school athletic team or squad with a predetermined roster, regular practices, and scheduled competitions with other elementary or secondary schools unless such student has submitted to the school principal a signed report from a licensed physician, licensed advanced practice registered nurse, or licensed physician assistant acting under the supervision of a licensed physician attesting that such student has, within the preceding 365 days, received both a physical examination and a mental health assessment. Current law only requires that the signed report attest that any such student has received a physical examination within the preceding 12 months. The bill prohibits any public elementary or secondary school from becoming a member of any organization or entity that regulates or governs interscholastic programs that does not deem eligible for participation any student who has satisfied the requirements for eligibility in accordance with the provisions of the bill. The provisions of the bill other than the requirement for the Board of Education to convene a work group have a delayed effective date of July 1, 2025. == Patron: Durant Employment of school protection officers in public schools. Permits any local law-enforcement agency to employ in any public elementary or secondary school in the local school division, pursuant to an agreement with the local school board, a school protection officer, defined in the bill as a retired law-enforcement officer hired by the local law-enforcement agency on a part-time basis to provide limited law-enforcement and security services to public elementary and secondary schools in the Commonwealth. The bill requires each such school board and local law-enforcement agency to enter into a memorandum of understanding that sets forth the powers and duties of school protection officers. The bill requires the Department of Criminal Justice Services to establish compulsory training standards for school protection officers and requires the collection of certain data relating to the activities of such officers. == Patron: Carroll Foy Donor human milk banks, health insurance; coverage for donor human milk. Prohibits any person from establishing or operating a donor human milk bank without first obtaining a license from the Department of Health. The bill directs the Department to establish criteria for the licensure of donor human milk banks and to promulgate regulations by July 1, 2025, that prohibit activities of donor human milk banks that do not meet the licensing standards and requirements The bill requires health insurers, corporations providing health care coverage subscription contracts, and health maintenance organizations to provide coverage for expenses incurred in the provision of pasteurized donor human milk. The bill specifies that the requirement applies if the covered person is an infant younger than the age of six months and a licensed medical practitioner has issued an order for such infant who satisfies certain criteria enumerated in the bill. The bill applies to policies, contracts, and plans delivered, issued for delivery, or renewed on or after January 1, 2025. The bill also requires the state plan for medical assistance services to include a provision for payment of medical assistance services incurred in the provision of pasteurized donor human milk. == Patron: Pekarsky Public elementary and secondary schools; student discipline; evidence-based restorative disciplinary practices. Prohibits, except in certain cases involving firearms, drugs, adjudications of delinquency, or convictions of certain crimes, any public elementary or secondary school student from being suspended, expelled, or excluded from attendance at school without consideration of first implementing at least one evidence-based restorative disciplinary practice such as community conferencing, community service, mentoring, a peer jury, peer mediation, positive behavioral interventions and supports, a restorative circle, or the Virginia Tiered Systems of Supports. == Patron: Mulchi K-12 schools and institutions of higher education; student participation in women's sports; civil cause of action. Requires each interscholastic, intercollegiate, intramural, or club athletic team or sport sponsored by a public school, or any other school that is a member of the Virginia High School League, or by a public institution of higher education to be expressly designated as one of the following based on the biological sex of the students who participate on the team or in the sport: (i) males, men, or boys; (ii) females, women, or girls; or (iii) coed or mixed if participation on such team or sport is open to both males and females. The bill prohibits any such team or sport that is expressly designated for females from being open to students whose biological sex is male. The bill also provides that in the event of a dispute as to the biological sex of any student seeking to participate on any interscholastic, intercollegiate, intramural, or club athletic team or sport that is expressly designated for males or females, such student may establish biological sex by presenting to the school or institution a signed physician's statement that attests to such student's biological sex based solely on (a) the student's internal and external reproductive anatomy; (b) the student's normal endogenously produced levels of testosterone; and (c) an analysis of the student's genetic makeup. The bill prohibits any government entity, licensing or accrediting organization, or athletic association or organization from entertaining a complaint, opening an investigation, or taking any other adverse action against any such school or institution of higher education based on a violation of the provisions of the bill and creates a cause of action for any school or institution of higher education that suffers harm as a result of a violation of the bill. Finally, the bill creates a civil cause of action for any student who suffers harm as a result of a knowing violation of a provision of the bill by a school or institution or as a result of the student's reporting a violation of a provision of the bill by a school, institution, athletic association, or organization. | | == | (none at this time) | | == | == == == == * == | | == | == Patron: Stanley Tow truck drivers and towing and recovery operators; prohibited acts; certain solicitation and offering of services; penalty. Prohibits tow truck drivers and towing and recovery operators from soliciting or offering towing services or causing any other person to solicit or offer towing services in any manner, directly or indirectly, at the scene of any wrecked or disabled motor vehicle upon a highway when such wrecked or disabled motor vehicle reasonably necessitates removal by a tow truck. The bill provides that a violation of such prohibition constitutes a Class 1 misdemeanor. == Patron: Bagby License plate readers; penalties. Provides requirements for the use of license plate readers, defined in the bill, by law-enforcement agencies. The bill requires such agencies to enter into an agreement with the license plate reader owners or other responsible non-law-enforcement entity to operate a data trust, defined in the bill, to store the data collected by a license plate reader and requires any such law-enforcement agency to apply to the data trust for access to such data. The bill limits the use of license plate readers to scanning, detecting, and identifying license plate numbers for the purpose of identifying vehicles involved in certain crimes. | == | no meeting | | == | no meeting | | == | no meeting | | == | no docket as of 6pm | WATCH OR COMMENT IN A SENATE COMMITTEE | | == | ACTION, ACTION, ACTION | Friends, Each house has until next Tuesday, February 13 at midnight before crossover, when all bills must be voted on by each respective house. name: =?utf-8?Q?Supervisor=20David=20Canepa?=
subject: January 2024 e-newsletter
body:
| | | |
---
| | |
# 2024 January e-newsletter
News and information for District 5 in San Mateo County.
---
|
---
|
---
| **San Mateo County first in nation to declare loneliness a public health crisis**
---
| Dear friends,

San Mateo County was the first in the nation to declare loneliness a public health crisis after the Board of Supervisors unanimously passed a resolution my office authored Tuesday, January 30.

Loneliness has a profound impact on health and is comparable to smoking a pack of cigarettes a day. It leads to poor health outcomes according to the U.S. Surgeon General Dr. Vivek Murthy such as heart attack, depression and even early death. That’s why I’m calling for the state of California to establish a Minister of Loneliness position to help those suffering in silence to restore meaningful connections that were lost during the pandemic.
Many people are suffering alone in silence and there’s no cure for it. Loneliness, isolation and lack of connection can lead to profound health impacts that send many of our most vulnerable residents on the path to homelessness, as we’ve seen in the county’s one-day homeless count conducted recently.
---
While there is no cure, there are ways we all can make a difference by extending love, support and real help to our neighbors, older adults and families before loneliness does become a crisis and leads to horrible outcomes such as suicide. They say loneliness can have the same health consequences as smoking a pack of cigarettes a day, an alarming fact that I think should be addressed by the state by establishing a Minister of Loneliness similar to what the UK and Japan have instituted.” The recent Surgeon General Advisory issued by Dr. Vivek Murthy underscores the severity of this issue, highlighting its profound impact on mental, physical, and societal health. In San Mateo County alone, 45% of residents reported experiencing difficulty with isolation and loneliness, with certain demographic groups facing heightened challenges in accessing social support networks. Here’s a link to an NBC story after the resolution passed: [http://nbcbay.com/dygsW1c](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=77bb35c6f9&e=c0babbc643)
---
| **Board of Supervisors picks new President, Vice President**

The Board of Supervisors unanimously voted in Warren Slocum as President of the Board of Supervisors Tuesday, Jan. 9 and I was proud to be voted in to serve as his Vice President for 2024 with the same unanimous vote.

President Slocum said the county has changed considerably since the time he first joined the Board in 2013.

He also said change is inevitable and required and called for amplifying public voices and strengthening the county’s equity work including passing an ordinance codifying the county’s commitment to it and “recalibrating” the Office of Equity and Social Justice. I look forward to advancing President Slocum’s vision this year and will continue to advance my own priorities of climate adaptation, fire mitigation, mental health and public safety.

Slocum replaces Dave Pine, who served as the Board’s President last year. I want to thank Supervisor Pine for his steady leadership last year as it started with unprecedented storms and flooding, followed shortly thereafter by the deadly mass shooting in Half Moon Bay.
---
---
| **
COVID-19 closed therapy pool, community finally opens it back up**

I was proud to announce the groundbreaking of Sutter Health’s project to reopen the Mack E. Mickelson Arthritis and Rehabilitation Center Therapeutic Pool in downtown San Mateo at Mills Health Center at a press conference I conducted on Saturday, Jan. 6.

Sutter announced in 2020 as the pandemic started that the pool would be closed permanently. Sutter now plans to reopen the pool in July 2024.

As many of you may know, the Mickelson warm water therapy pool is the only facility in San Mateo County that provides a wheelchair-accessible warm pool specifically designed for therapeutic use by seniors, adults and children with chronic pain or disabilities, patients recovering from surgery, pregnant women, and others who find the warm water healing.

Shortly after the pool was closed nearly four years ago, Lindsay Raike formed the nonprofit advocacy group Warm Water Wellness and coordinated a grassroots effort with the support of my office to reopen the pool. With her support, I authored a resolution in February, 2022 urging Sutter to reopen the pool that the San Mateo County Board of Supervisors unanimously passed.

Many in need of this warm water therapy pool were told it would close forever but Sutter has listened to the community and is keeping its promise to rehabilitate Mickelson and open it back up to the community in July. It wasn’t easy and took a coordinated grassroots effort to make this happen and I applaud the hundreds and even thousands in our community who did not give up, who did not take “no” for an answer.
---
| **
UCSF conference highlights emergency preparedness**

I was honored to be the keynote speaker at the 2024 UCSF Campus Center of Excellence, COE conference 8.0, Monday Jan. 29. The theme of this year’s four-day conference was organizational resilience.

UCSF is one of the world’s most renowned public institutions and the leading university dedicated to advancing health worldwide through preeminent biomedical research, graduate-level education in the life sciences and health professions and excellence in patient care.

But none of that could be achieved without the nearly 500 employees in Facilities Services, the trade workers in operations, custodial services, maintenance, landscaping, public safety and engineering.

It is their task to keep UCSF’s facilities and operations resilient, sustainable and prepared for any emergency.
>From severe storms and flooding, deadly diseases and active shooter scenarios, it takes coordination across several departments to ensure the facilities, staff and patients are safe in any emergency situation.

I applaud Cesar Sanchez, the Assistant Vice Chancellor of Facilities Services and his team for putting this incredible conference together with an emphasis on sustaining the 43,000 jobs and $8.9 billion in economic activity UCSF generates annually.
---
| **Interns wanted to join our team ** We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=405c793530&e=c0babbc643). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org)
---
| **Battling food insecurity **
Food insecurity is a problem some residents may still face as we continue to emerge from the pandemic. Here are some resources residents can access: **CalFresh:**
Is a program for people with low-income who meet federal income eligibility rules and want to add to their budget to put healthy and nutritious food on the table. Website: [https://www.cdss.ca.gov/food-nutrition/calfresh](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=79573598d1&e=c0babbc643) Contact: (650) 802-6576, [antorres@smcgov.org](mailto:antorres@smcgov.org) **Daly City Partnership:**
DC Partnership is a local partnership that was formed to guide collaborative efforts and connect our community to services that promote well-being. Check out their website for times and locations. Website: [https://www.dcpartnership.org/covid19/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=5f53343162&e=c0babbc643) **Second Harvest Food Bank:**
Second Harvest offers food at various locations throughout San Mateo County listed on its webpage. Website: [https://www.shfb.org/get-food/](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=accac85edc&e=c0babbc643) Contacts: Call (800) 984-3663 or text GETFOOD to (408) 455-5181 **Interns wanted to join our team **

We are looking to add to our great group of interns we are currently working with. If you or someone you know may be interested in applying to my Internship Program, submitting this form is the first step: [https://forms.gle/hiKqtn7Rird5vXwB9](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=b2fc1fea55&e=c0babbc643). You can also reach out to Mike Richardson on my staff directly with any questions at [mrichardson@smcgov.org](mailto:mrichardson@smcgov.org) In friendship,
David J. Canepa
---
|
---
|
---
|
---
| | |
---
| _Copyright © 2024 Supervisor David Canepa, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Supervisor David Canepa1618 Sullivan497Daly City, CA 94015
[Add us to your address book](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/vcard?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/profile?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&e=c0babbc643&c=dc0c617bdb) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://davidcanepa.us1.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&id=ea541c4b96&t=b&e=c0babbc643&c=dc0c617bdb).

[](http://www.mailchimp.com/email-referral/?utm_source=freemium_newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=referral_marketing&aid=a3c7592558e548bc8cc150aa5&afl=1)
---

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: BREAKING: McConnell’s super PAC makes massive Ohio ad buy
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
#
Derek, it’s Sherrod Brown, and I’ll be quick.
**News just broke that a Mitch McConnell-aligned super PAC is reserving nearly $83M of TV airtime in Ohio ALONE to attack my record and flip this seat red.**
**This is the largest reservation ever made by this group in a single state.**
**[I know we can fight back and win this race, but not without your help. So please, will you split a donation now between me and John Fetterman to fight back against Mitch McConnell, win in Ohio, and keep the Senate blue?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20240207-1?refcode=em20240207-1-8784&refcode2=8784_434096_3iiGW1&akid=8784%2E434096%2E3iiGW1)**
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-email-20240207-1?amount=5&refcode=em20240207-8784&refcode2=8784_434096_3iiGW1&akid=8784%2E434096%2E3iiGW1)**
Thanks for your support at this crucial moment.
– Sherrod
U.S. Senator, Ohio

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jf-emails?refcode=em20240207-8784-footer&t=1&refcode2=8784_434096_3iiGW1&akid=8784%2E434096%2E3iiGW1)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/JohnFettermanPA/) | [](https://twitter.com/johnfetterman) | [](https://www.instagram.com/johnfetterman/)
Email us: info@johnfetterman.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Fetterman for PA, PO Box 6061, Pittsburgh PA 15211.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.johnfetterman.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.johnfetterman.com/sign/personal_info/?t=2&akid=8784%2E434096%2E3iiGW1)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.johnfetterman.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=3&akid=8784%2E434096%2E3iiGW1)

clean_body: Incoming Message <> Action Required | -INCOMING MESSAGE FROM THE GOP- Internal Survey Report. Intended for: Please Read Below. Peter, it's great to meet you. I wanted to send you some preliminary information regarding the GOP's efforts to Take Back the Senate in 2024. We're surveying to gauge voters' interests in the windup to the November election. This election is all-or-nothing. If we win, we'll have a chance at saving this nation, but if we lose, it could all be for nothing. We just have a few questions for you, and anything you can answer will help us -- please read the survey below! Best, 2024 Official GOP Survey -OFFICIAL GOP SURVEY- Friend, we just have a few questions for you. Attached are various questions that will help us understand how our voter base feels. The only thing you have to do is click the buttons and give your honest opinion. This is all COMPLETELY anonymous. Polls like these help us craft a winning strategy for the 2024 election, and your response is sure to help us fight back against the liberal democrats and TAKE BACK AMERICA. We 're counting on your response! Question 1 - I Intend to vote in the GOP Primary | Question 2 - I am more politically active than in 2020. Question 3 - The economy is heading in the wrong direction. Question 4 - The Biden administration 's foreign policy is wrong for America. Question 5 - The country is heading in the wrong direction. Question 6 - I describe the situation on the border as a crisis. That 's it for now -- thank you for your response! PAID FOR BY DOLAN FOR OHIO Not authorized by any other candidate.

| | I'll never forget the first time I was elected to the Nebraska Legislature in 2016. I'll never forget because I was so humbled by the way voters rallied behind my campaign. We knocked tens of thousands of doors, made countless calls, and won by taking our message directly to people. I'm hoping you'll give that same energy to my campaign for Congress [**— because right now, I need your support more than ever before.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78586210/453831606/1698357921?refcode=20240113_em_fr_b1_ntony_ia&amount=15&amounts=15,50,100,250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2Q2YTQxMy1lYmM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjM4NDgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81FiCk9g7dtcZMVfSB1EcyHqYiX_qLn9k1Rhc5EadKk=) [**_To be honest with you, my race is emerging as one of the most consequential fights in our effort to retake the House from Republican extremists. Flipping Nebraska’s Second Congressional district could put Democrats back in control and end this reign of chaos. Not only that, it would mean electing a strong, capable representative that Nebraska families deserve to fight for them. So please, could you send in $15 to my campaign before my midnight deadline? >>_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78586213/453831642/1698357921?refcode=20240113_em_fr_b1_ntony_ia&amount=15&amounts=15,50,100,250&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS85MDE0NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlN2Q2YTQxMy1lYmM1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjM4NDgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=81FiCk9g7dtcZMVfSB1EcyHqYiX_qLn9k1Rhc5EadKk=) I’ll be frank with you: Everything is on the line in this election. From lowering healthcare costs, to protecting Medicare and Social Security, to restoring abortion rights, we can’t afford to let Bacon’s extreme allies buy him another win. [**_That’s why I’m sincerely asking: Will you send in $15 — or whatever you can spare — before my midnight deadline? When the FEC reports were released, one thing was abundantly clear: Our campaign is people-powered, while our opponent's is powered by her wealthiest and best-connected friends.

We received tens of thousands of donations to our campaign and 92% were $100 or less. In comparison, more than 80% of what our main opponent raised were from donations of $3,300 or more.

It's clear who the grassroots, everyday people are choosing in this primary: A new poll showed us with a double-digit lead despite our opponent's endless war chest and political connections.

The people of New Jersey don't want to be told who to vote for, they want to change our broken politics. But here's the truth: There are still over 100 days left until this election is over, and we know our opponent will use every lever of power she has to let the political elite decide this election instead of voters.

We need to keep up our momentum and build a campaign that can win in the face of big money and a rigged political system. Can we count on you to pitch in today to win this race? body:
“THE SCREW AMERICA BILL” – Mark Levin

| |

---
| | | **Mark Levin DROPS THE HAMMER on the weak Senate Border Bill:**

_**" THE SCREW AMERICA BILL. How many Democrats and media propagandists who support this monstrous bill have actually read it? I just finished going through it and it is far worse than even I imagined... They try to sneak things through, including granting the DC courts jurisdiction over immigration matters - that would be the leftist judges in Washington."**_

MARK SPEAKS THE TRUTH!

This bill allows MILLIONS of illegal immigrants to cross our border every year, grants automatic work permits to asylum seekers, and does NOTHING to actually secure our border.

Any border bill that encourages illegal immigration should go down in flames. _**[That's why we're calling for a UNIVERSAL declaration from Conservatives everywhere to bring down this bill once and for all.](http://email.alert.keepcongressrepublican.com/c/eJyUkTGP3CAUhH8N7rDggeFcuDjl5Ca6KEqTEmF450UL2ALsu58feaWt0iT1m_lm9Ob3u3m3IYa8_rAJp7srplTTTlOdjfRmU8JigIGkDCjThnd-8qNTVnU4cc20EMPLwLvb9OG1X5yzemQfEmCRgAuCU4wJKSWILkwXhwHTfGRSqv7Fa-YQYHTaL9p7IpmNWFp_R9zdlteCtRbcjyUGZ3PvttTF6dbaXol4JTATmCu6o2D_GXJBfykIzA1tolf_UPEhybYhXbbisdAlxEgjniFfADGXC1BCXol4a-VAAupoyTibdhvWTMTbs7X5-Uq_f_vFe86FkmAal3LQQlOuB0VA4Zdpwf-H4ZGz5Ya5XeEnvbtCn2YC6uvI59-Hrkx-_wwxhqo0kWxNNsTHb9o_zPcnAAD__xeumEc "That’s why we’re calling for a UNIVERSAL declaration from Conservatives everywhere to bring down this bill once and for all.")**_

---
Look:

Joe Biden and the open-border Democrats are fully responsible for the border crisis. This Senate Bill doesn't include any serious ways to fix it. It would do NOTHING to end catch-and-release, NOTHING to force Biden's hand to slash illegal immigration, and NOTHING to finish The Wall.

Thankfully, Majority Leader Steve Scalise issued a blistering statement saying he won't even bring it up for a vote in the House.

body:
This would mean a lot to Kevin | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78587572/453837438/1420976906?refcode=RE_EM_PET_240207&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzAvMS84OTU5NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0Nzk0NzdjOS1lY2M1LWVlMTEtYjY2MC0wMDIyNDgyMjM4NDgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=3k3_vt7pMYEACy9FDiq8soHh9vsJWp77xaH3ezn0wVM=&emci=3ab0c563-e9c5-ee11-b660-002248223848&emdi=479477c9-ecc5-ee11-b660-002248223848&ceid=37077096)

There’s a good reason we’re working so hard to elect Kevin Thomas to Congress, team. Republicans like Kevin’s opponent in NY-04 have ignored the needs of hardworking families in favor of their own political agendas.

We know it's been time to make a change, but the people in power need to know why, and we want them to hear that directly from voters like you.

name: Debbie Mucarsel-Powell
subject: a different kind of love letter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Debbie for Florida
body:
I hope you'll take the time to read this.
| | |
---
Friend, this message is a bit on the longer side, but I hope you'll take the time to read it. **[You see, in a lot of ways, this campaign is a love letter to Florida.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78588250/453840026/1820369458?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk752-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDBkMWRmOTUtZWVjNS1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzODQ4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ZYMMtU8is6Wgrccjoo9w8PnbfQs14z14du4G8bpsLI8=)**
I can still remember the first time that I came to Florida. I was working on my Masters degree and had just gotten the news that my beloved father had been killed by a criminal with a gun in Ecuador, where I was born. As I grappled with my grief, I found myself missing Ecuador more than I had in years. I missed its warmth, lush greenery, and close-knit communities that I grew up with. My older sister, Monica, knew our family was struggling, so she convinced me to visit her in Miami, where she had recently moved.
When I stepped outside of the airport, it was like stepping into another world. **It was warm, wonderfully humid, bustling with lively, diverse communities, lush, and all so, so beautifully green.** We spent the next few days visiting all of Monica's favorite places in her new city — the spots with the most beautiful views and the best cafes to get a _cafecito_. The people we met were even warmer than the weather. Everyone was welcoming, friendly, and proud to call this state their home.
I boarded my plane back to my home with a heavy heart but a new resolve. **As soon as I was able, I sold my car and moved across the country to make a new home in Miami.**(My mom, always my closest confidant, wasn't far behind.)
Since moving here all those years ago, I've had the privilege to build a beautiful life in the Sunshine State. **I fell in love here, found the community I had longed for, raised a family of my own, and dedicated my career to serving this state.** I've helped protect the Everglades, expanded access to health care, worked for Florida International University, and became the first South American immigrant elected to Congress, right here in Florida.
I've spent my life serving this state and getting to know the communities who call it home. But my opponent, Rick Scott, has spent his career scamming Floridians, lying to voters, and making himself richer. **He wants you to think that Florida families agree with his extreme, hateful agenda._But I can promise you, friend, that couldn't be further from the truth._**
Listen, my campaign is deeply personal because of my love for this state — and more importantly, because of what it will mean for my fellow Floridians. Rick Scott wants to sow division and undo decades of meaningful progress. He wants to destroy reproductive rights, gun safety laws, and vital public services, like Medicare and Social Security, that support millions of his constituents, including my own mother.

Florida is ready for a change — I hear it from voters every day as I travel around all 67 counties. I know this November, Floridians will choose unity, progress, and kindness over Rick Scott's extremist agenda.

But the truth is, we're up against a mega-millionaire who spent $64 million of his personal fortune to buy this seat last time. If we're going to break through Rick Scott's lies, build unstoppable grassroots momentum, and win this critical race, I'll need your help.

**[Please, will you donate $5 (or whatever you can spare today) to help me defeat Rick Scott and give our home the leadership that we deserve? This _will_ be the year that we flip Florida blue, but only if grassroots activists like _you_ believe in my campaign.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78588251/453840027/1820369458?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk752-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDBkMWRmOTUtZWVjNS1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzODQ4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ZYMMtU8is6Wgrccjoo9w8PnbfQs14z14du4G8bpsLI8=)**
_If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | | | [DONATE $5](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78588252/453840028/1820369458?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&express_lane=true&amount=5&refcode=jtk752-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDBkMWRmOTUtZWVjNS1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzODQ4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ZYMMtU8is6Wgrccjoo9w8PnbfQs14z14du4G8bpsLI8=)

Thank you for taking the time to read this and fight for a better future for Florida.

With gratitude,

Debbie
|
* * *
| | | [Contribute](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/78588256/453840032/-31196584?refcode=jtk752-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS8xMDc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZDBkMWRmOTUtZWVjNS1lZTExLWI2NjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzODQ4IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=ZYMMtU8is6Wgrccjoo9w8PnbfQs14z14du4G8bpsLI8=)
---
Debbie Mucarsel-Powell is a Floridian, South American immigrant, mother, and former Congresswoman. She’s dedicated her life to uplifting working families, ending gun violence, and fighting for her community. Now, she’s running to defeat Republican Senator Rick Scott and win a better, brighter future for Florida.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
